VAL-AUTH-001.evidence: mors init + mors login --invite-token <token> --json: exit code 0, output {"status":"authenticated","account_id":"<hex32>","device_id":"device-<uuid>"}. No GitHub URLs, device codes, verification URIs, OAuth references in output. Account ID is hex-derived from invite token (not GitHub user ID). Session token is mors-session.* format (not gh*_ OAuth token).

VAL-AUTH-002.evidence: After login, separate process `mors status --json --offline` returns {"status":"authenticated","account_id":"<same-hex32>"} with matching account_id. `mors status --json` (with liveness) returns token_valid:true. Session file persists across process boundaries.

VAL-AUTH-007.evidence: 1) Empty dir (no init): exit code 1, missing=["invite_token","device_keys","initialized"], message includes remediation for each. 2) After init, no invite token: exit code 1, missing=["invite_token"], remediation mentions --invite-token. 3) Non-JSON mode: stderr includes 'Missing required authentication prerequisites' with remediation steps.

VAL-AUTH-011.evidence: 1) Invalid invite-token format rejected: exit code 1, error=invalid_invite_token, message mentions format. 2) Valid invite-token + device keys: exit code 0, status=authenticated, session persisted. 3) Invite token via MORS_INVITE_TOKEN env var: exit code 0, status=authenticated. 4) Absent device keys rejected with missing_prerequisites. Initial bug found and fixed: CLI checked for wrong device key filenames (device.pub/device.key instead of device-keys.json/x25519.key/ed25519.key). Fix: replaced inline file check with canonical isDeviceBootstrapped() function.

frictions.description: CLI login command checked for legacy device key filenames (device.pub, device.key) instead of the real filenames created by mors init (device-keys.json, x25519.key, ed25519.key). This caused all login attempts after real mors init to fail with missing_prerequisites. Fixed by using the canonical isDeviceBootstrapped() function.
